Celebrating the Journey

Birth
reimagined.
For you.

Birth Loop is a full immersive board game experience where you eat, play, laugh and learn everything you need to feel truly ready for birth. No boring lectures. No clipboards. Just real preparation that actually sticks — and a gamified innovation in reducing maternal inequalities in our communities.

Trauma-Informed Practice
Book Your Place Learn More

Celebrating the Journey

What to Expect

This is not a class.
This is an experience.

Part escape room. Part celebration. Part life-changing preparation. Birth Loop brings together everything you need to feel confident, informed and ready.

Escape Room Energy

Navigate a giant, tactile board game with real challenges, live scenarios and decisions that actually matter. Feel the thrill of problem-solving your way through birth preparation.

Choose Your Own Path

No two journeys are the same. You pick your entry point, choose which areas matter most to you, and navigate the board your way. Homebirth? C-section? VBAC? It's your game.

Real Experts in the Room

A midwife, a professional counsellor, and peer supporters who've been through it themselves are with you the whole time. Ask anything. Nothing is off limits.

Food, Drink and Good Vibes

Culturally sensitive food and drink flows throughout the session. It's a celebration as much as it is preparation. Come hungry, leave full in every way.

Leave With a Toolkit

Every participant leaves with a physical Confidence Toolkit packed with birth and postpartum essentials, vouchers, and all the signposting you need for what comes next.

Completely Free

No cost. No catch. Childcare, travel expenses and food are all covered. Birth Loop removes every barrier so that every family in South London can access the preparation they deserve.

300+ Families to be supported

About Birth Loop

Where birth preparation becomes a celebration

Imagine an escape room designed around your birth journey. Birth Loop is a large-scale, immersive board game experience that transforms how families prepare for one of the biggest moments of their lives. Think tactile challenges, live scenarios, problem-solving under pressure and real midwife expertise — all in one celebratory space.

You navigate a colour-coded game board, choose your own path through the maternity journey, and unlock knowledge through play. Food and drink are provided throughout, culturally sensitive and delicious. You leave with a Confidence Toolkit, vouchers, and everything you need to feel ready.

Trauma-Informed DBS-Checked Team Free to Attend NHS-Linked
Explore Sessions

What We Offer

Sessions designed
around you

Book Your Spot

The Birth Loop Game Event

Our flagship immersive experience — a large-scale board game where you navigate the full maternity journey through tactile challenges, peer learning, and real-world scenarios. Using Play-Doh, Dingbats, and Learning Counters to make every concept accessible.

Women-Only Couples Men-Only South London

Postnatal Peer Support

A community-led, peer-supported space for families in the early postnatal period. Connect with others who truly understand the journey, access lived-experience guidance, and find your footing in those precious early weeks.

Peer-Led Community Space All Welcome
Linked to MNVP for ongoing NHS improvement →

All sessions are completely free and fully supported

Confidence Toolkit

Why Birth Loop

Why families believe in this

Birth Loop hasn't launched yet — but the need is clear and the belief is real. Here's what people are saying about why this matters.

Expectant Parent "
I wish something like this existed when I was pregnant with my first. I had no idea what questions to ask or what I was entitled to. Birth Loop fills a gap that NHS appointments simply can't.
Amara T. Expectant mum, Brixton
Father "
As a dad, birth education has never felt designed for me. The men's session concept is exactly what's missing — practical, jargon-free, and action-oriented. I want to feel useful, not lost.
Marcus D. Expectant father, Peckham
Community Midwife "
The families I work with in South London face real inequalities in maternity outcomes. A game-based, community-rooted approach that meets people where they are is not just innovative — it's necessary.
Priya R. Community Midwife, Lambeth

Reserve Your Place

Book the Game Event

All sessions are free to attend and run for 4 hours on Saturdays. Our first session is on Saturday 16 May in Brixton. Places are limited — secure yours early. We'll confirm within 24 hours and share full event details including location and what to bring.

Our Partners & Funders

Supported by those who
believe in the journey

Questions

Frequently asked

All Birth Loop game events are completely free to attend, including childcare, travel support, food, and a Confidence Toolkit goody bag to take home.
Events are currently running in Brixton and Peckham, South London. We are expanding to more areas soon — get in touch to register your interest for other locations.
Every event includes a qualified Midwife & Facilitator, a BACP-accredited Professional Counsellor, and Peer Supporters with lived maternity experience. All staff are DBS checked and trained in trauma-informed practice.
Yes. We offer Women-Only, Couples, and Men-Only formats — each thoughtfully adapted for the group. Select your preferred format when booking.
Birth Loop works alongside the Maternity and Neonatal Voices Partnership (MNVP). Anonymous insights generated through game sessions feed directly into NHS service improvement, ensuring your voice shapes maternity care in your local trust.
Yes — our approach was specifically designed to be inclusive. We use tactile tools like Play-Doh, visual puzzles, and physical counters to bypass language and literacy barriers. Language support is also available — just let us know your needs when booking.

We use essential cookies to make this site work. We'd also like to set optional analytics cookies to help us improve it. Read our Cookie Policy to learn more. Birth Loop is operated by Learn Loop Ltd.

Cookie Policy

Birth Loop · Operated by Learn Loop Ltd

This Cookie Policy explains how Birth Loop, operated by Learn Loop Ltd, uses cookies and similar technologies when you visit birthloop.co.uk. By using our website, you agree to the use of cookies as described in this policy.

What Are Cookies?

Cookies are small text files placed on your device when you visit a website. They help the site remember your preferences and understand how you use it, making your experience smoother and more relevant.

Who We Are

Birth Loop is a community programme operated by Learn Loop Ltd, a company registered in England and Wales. Our website is birthloop.co.uk. If you have any questions about how we use cookies, you can contact us at [email protected].

The Cookies We Use

Essential Cookies

These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off. They are usually set in response to actions you take, such as filling in a booking form or setting your privacy preferences.

  • session_id — Keeps your form session active while you complete a booking
  • cookie_consent — Remembers your cookie preferences so we don't ask again

Analytics Cookies (Optional)

With your permission, we use analytics cookies to understand how visitors interact with our site — for example, which pages are most visited and how people navigate to our booking form. This helps us improve the experience for everyone.

  • _ga, _gid — Google Analytics cookies that help us understand site traffic anonymously. No personally identifiable information is collected.

Third-Party Cookies

Our booking form is powered by Formspree. When you submit a booking, Formspree may set its own cookies to process your submission securely. Please refer to Formspree's privacy policy for details.

How Long Do Cookies Last?

  • Essential cookies — Last for your browser session or up to 12 months
  • Analytics cookies — Up to 24 months
  • cookie_consent — 12 months, so we remember your choice

Your Choices

You can choose to accept all cookies or essential cookies only using the banner at the bottom of this page. You can also change or withdraw your consent at any time by clearing your browser cookies and revisiting the site.

Most browsers also allow you to control cookies through their settings. Visit aboutcookies.org for guidance on how to manage cookies in your browser.

Changes to This Policy

We may update this Cookie Policy from time to time to reflect changes in technology or legal requirements. The latest version will always be available on this page. Last updated: March 2025.

Contact Us

If you have any questions about this Cookie Policy or how Learn Loop Ltd handles your data, please contact us at [email protected].
